contract.goal: Scaffold bin/guild CLI with flat-verb dispatcher mirroring bin/draft and bin/griot. Port .claude/scripts/guild/parse-and-aggregate.ts to .claude/cli/verbs/guild/parse-and-aggregate.ts. Byte-equivalent behavior. Also fix an EAGAIN bug in both dispatchers (bin/griot + bin/guild) discovered during D1 smoke testing — the eager readFileSync(0) call now catches EAGAIN and treats it as empty stdin. D1 of 5 in Phase 2.
acceptance_criteria: bin/guild shim exists, modeled on bin/griot/bin/draft.
.claude/cli/guild.ts is a flat-verb dispatcher (same shape as .claude/cli/griot.ts); registers parse-and-aggregate verb.
.claude/cli/verbs/guild/parse-and-aggregate.ts exports parseAndAggregateVerb(rest, ctx); byte-equivalent to legacy.
EAGAIN fix applied to both dispatchers (.claude/cli/griot.ts + .claude/cli/guild.ts).
npm run lint clean; npm test clean; byte parity confirmed via diff against legacy script.
rules_applied: Mirror Phase 1's bin/griot architecture (flat verb dispatcher, sibling tests, ValidationError class for cross-function flow).
L-002 carve-out: in-scope scope expansion declared upfront in contract (the EAGAIN fix to bin/griot) — not silent.
disqualifiers: Modify or delete legacy .claude/scripts/guild/parse-and-aggregate.ts or .test.ts (D5's scope).
Diverge from bin/draft/bin/griot's flat-verb dispatcher architecture.
Hand-edit substrate JSON outside CLI verbs (manifest backfill is a documented, separately-committed precursor).

actions: Cut substrate-cli/phase-2 branch from origin/main (e0facac, post #99 squash merge + a small loom-archive SKILL.md update from another author).

Hand-edit manifest backfill (precursor commit 7cbf0fd): added phases 2-6 rows with status=not-started; marked Phase 1 PR state as merged. One-time substrate workaround — bin/loom has no `phase add` verb; that's the deferred substrate-gap sibling project.
bin/loom phase update substrate-cli 2 --status=in-progress --branch=substrate-cli/phase-2 (emits phase-started event).
Wrote .claude/cli/guild.ts dispatcher (147 lines, mirror of griot.ts: VERBS map, parseInvocation, formatHelp, formatUnknownVerbError, dispatch, main, isEntryPoint).
Wrote .claude/cli/verbs/guild/index.ts (37 lines, exports GUILD_VERBS, GuildCliContext, DispatchResult, GuildVerbHandler).
Wrote .claude/cli/verbs/guild/parse-and-aggregate.ts (217 lines, ported from legacy with ValidationError class replacing the legacy fail-then-process.exit pattern).

Discovered EAGAIN bug during smoke test: bin/guild --help (and bin/griot --help) crashed when invoked in a bash compound after a prior command consumed the parent's stdin pipe. Reproduced and confirmed. Fixed both dispatchers with try/catch around the readFileSync(0) call; EAGAIN treated as empty stdin (the right interpretation for help/parseInvocation paths).
Wrote .claude/cli/verbs/guild/parse-and-aggregate.test.ts (183 lines, 18 tests adapted from legacy).
Wrote .claude/cli/guild.test.ts (156 lines, 14 dispatcher tests + spawnSync smoke tests).
Verified EAGAIN fix: bin/guild --help and bin/griot --help no longer crash in compound bash contexts.
Ran npm test: 36 files / 481 tests pass (was 34/449; +2 files +32 tests).
Spawned evaluator-contract-fit + evaluator-naming directly in parallel (panel composition per PANEL-COMPOSITION.md for .claude/cli/**/*.ts files). Both VERDICT: approved.

scope: Fix EAGAIN-on-empty-stdin in both dispatchers (scope expansion, declared upfront).
changes_since_previous: First checkin on substrate-cli/phase-2 branch. Baseline: origin/main at e0facac, post #99 merge of Phase 1 + a small loom-archive SKILL.md update from another author. Precursor commit on this branch: 7cbf0fd (manifest backfill).
notes_for_pr: D1 of 5 in Phase 2. PR collects all 5 deliverables before review.

Precursor commit on this branch (7cbf0fd): manifest backfill for phases 2-6. One-time substrate workaround for the missing bin/loom phase add verb (the loom-side substrate gap is deferred to a sibling project per PLAN.md § Scope > Deferred). Manifest will revert to CLI-only updates once the sibling-project ships.

EAGAIN fix shipped in both bin/griot and bin/guild dispatchers. Discovered during D1 smoke testing — same bug existed in bin/griot since Phase 1's D1 (shipped in #99) but only triggers in bash compounds where a prior command consumed the parent's stdin pipe. The fix catches EAGAIN inside the eager-stdin block and treats it as empty stdin (the right interpretation for verbs that don't consume stdin like help/use; verbs that do consume stdin will fail their own downstream 'empty input' check).
Byte-for-byte parity verified for parse-and-aggregate via diff against legacy script.

Naming evaluator surfaced one observation (not a flag): the <verb>Verb suffix on handlers (parseAndAggregateVerb) is mildly redundant when paired with the verbs/ directory. This is the established Phase 1 convention; sibling-consistency wins. Worth noting for Phase 3's audit but not changing now.
